title: isoetes velata subsp. asturicensis
intro: Isoetes velata subsp. asturicensis, also known as Asturian quillwort, is a rare and endangered species of aquatic plant that belongs to the quillwort family (Isoetaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits associated with Isoetes velata subsp. asturicensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the rhizomes and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of these preparations.
